operation: First, initialize a workplane on the XY plane. Sketch a closed 2D profile as follows: start at the origin (0,0), draw a 15-unit horizontal line to (15,0), create a 3-point arc passing through (20,5) that ends at (15,10), draw a 15-unit horizontal line left to (0,10), then close the sketch path. Extrude this closed profile 8 units along the Z-axis to produce a 3D solid. Finally, select all edges at the maximum X and minimum X positions on faces parallel to the Z axis, and apply a 3-unit chamfer to these selected edges.
geometry: This is a uniform-thickness prismatic solid, with a cross-section that features three straight edges and one outward-convex curved end. One end of the prism is flat and perpendicular to its length direction, while the opposite end is smoothly rounded. The lengthwise edges at both the straight and curved ends are chamfered, creating angled bevel faces that connect the top, bottom, and side surfaces of the prism and remove all sharp outer edges.
function: This part is intended for use as a low-profile wear pad, fixture locating stop, or ergonomic pushbutton. Its rounded end minimizes abrasion during sliding contact, while chamfered edges eliminate sharp corners to simplify assembly, improve handling safety, and reduce stress concentration under load.
